questions.question: Which media query targets screens with a minimum width of 768px?
questions.explanation: @media (min-width: 768px) applies styles when the viewport is 768px or wider.

questions.question: Which media type targets styles specifically for printed pages?
questions.explanation: The print media type applies styles only when the document is being printed or shown in print preview.

questions.question: How do you combine two conditions in a media query so both must be true?
questions.explanation: The 'and' keyword combines multiple media features so styles apply only when all conditions are met.

questions.question: In a mobile-first responsive design, which approach is preferred?
choices: Use max-width queries to override desktop styles for mobile
Use min-width queries to progressively add styles for larger screens
Use only the screen media type without width queries
Write separate CSS files for each screen size
questions.explanation: Mobile-first design starts with base styles for small screens and uses min-width media queries to add styles as the viewport grows.

questions.question: A media query begins with the ___ keyword in CSS.
questions.explanation: @media is the at-rule used to define media queries that conditionally apply CSS rules.

questions.question: The ___ media feature tests whether the viewport width is at most a given value.
questions.explanation: max-width applies styles when the viewport width is equal to or less than the specified value.
